w20: Cost-effective entry-level shared-core instance featuring AMD EPYC Genoa architecture for lightweight, budget-conscious computing tasks and basic web serving applications.
c150: Hetzner Cloud cpx12 is a cost-effective, entry-level shared CPU instance featuring an AMD EPYC Genoa processor, 2GB of RAM, and 40GB of local SSD storage.
c200: The Hetzner Cloud cpx12 is a shared-core instance utilizing an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. It provides 1 vCPU, 2GB of memory, and 40GB of SSD storage, optimized for lightweight, cost-sensitive workloads.
w150: The Hetzner Cloud cpx12 is a shared-core instance built on the x86_64 AMD EPYC Genoa architecture. It provides 1 virtual CPU at 2.0 GHz, 2 GB of memory, and 40 GB of local SSD storage. The hardware supports AVX-512 instruction sets, including F, VNNI, and BF16, alongside AES and SHA-NI for cryptographic acceleration. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core performance relative to its class, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics reflect its entry-level positioning. With no dedicated GPU, the server is designed for resource-efficient operations. Priced at 0.0096 EUR per hour, this configuration is suitable for lightweight tasks such as static web serving, small-scale database operations, or basic CI/CD pipelines where cost efficiency is prioritized over high-concurrency throughput. Its architecture and performance profile make it a functional choice for low-demand applications requiring standard x86 compatibility without the overhead of larger, multi-core deployments.
